# EyeRest - 20-20-20 Rule Eye Care Extension

EyeRest is a premium, privacy-focused Chrome extension designed to reduce digital eye strain using the scientifically proven 20-20-20 rule.

## Purpose
Helps users prevent Computer Vision Syndrome (CVS), dry eyes, and fatigue by reminding them to take 20-second breaks every 20 minutes to look at something 20 feet away.

## Key Features
- **Native Alarm Integration**: Uses Chrome's `chrome.alarms` API for ultra-low battery and CPU usage.
- **Privacy-First**: Zero data collection, no accounts, and works entirely offline.
- **Guided Exercises**: Animated focal-shift exercises to relax eye muscles during breaks.
- **Adaptive UI**: Beautiful glassmorphism design that matches OS Light/Dark mode preferences.
- **Customizable**: Adjustable intervals, snooze lengths, and notification styles.

## Links
- [Chrome Web Store](https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/eyerest-20-20-20-screen-t/kkhnggiamacckgpdkmeecjclididdloi?authuser=0&hl=en-GB)
- [Official Website](https://eyerest.brilworks.com/)
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/drdhavaltrivedi/EyeRest-Chrome-Extension)
- [Privacy Policy](https://eyerest.brilworks.com/privacy.html)

## Frequently Asked Questions
- **What is the 20-20-20 rule?** Every 20 minutes, look 20 feet away for 20 seconds.
- **Does it collect data?** No. Everything stays on your local device.
- **Is it open source?** Yes, MIT Licensed.
